beats.narration: Touch a paper towel to spilled coffee — it climbs in, uphill, no pump. Penetrant testing hires that same force.

payload.title: CAPILLARY ACTION
payload.sub: One force, hired twice — in during dwell, out during development.

beats.narration: Inside the liquid, molecules pull on each other — cohesion, making surface tension. Adhesion pulls liquid toward the solid. The winner decides whether it wets.
A good penetrant wets clean metal, spreading almost flat. Stand it in a fine tube and it climbs — narrower passage, harder pull.

payload.heading: Tighter paths pull harder
payload.note: NARROWER PASSAGE → STRONGER PULL → A CRACK IS A FLATTENED TUBE

beats.narration: So the tightest cracks grab penetrant strongest, and gravity barely matters — vertical or overhead fills the same. Viscosity and cold set speed, not entry.

payload.heading: What the rule means at the part

chips.s: Hardest to see, easiest to fill

beats.narration: Two questions run the whole method. Can the penetrant get in? Did you give it enough time? Answer both, and PT makes sense.

payload.sub: Wetting and openness, then dwell — the whole method in two questions.
